_comment: SPECIALIST FLEET — three models GENUINELY DIFFERENT IN KIND, not three generalists from three labs. This is the fleet the whole project has never actually tested: prior fleets were peer generalists (Qwen3-32B/Gemma-3-27B/Mistral-24B), and every null (judge, ensemble, router) was measured on THAT homogeneity. This fleet swaps in real specialists so the Phase-1 gate can ask, with the QUERIES HELD FIXED (the frozen 30 hard queries), whether genuine specialization produces the divergence the peer fleet lacked. DEVICE PINNING: each model gets its own 80GB card via the new `device` field in boot.sh (0-based CUDA index) — these are ~52-64GB each and CANNOT co-reside. Ports avoid 8001 (RunPod proxy squats on it). Availability risk: a 3x80GB pod may be capacity-scarce; boot.sh checks nvidia-smi FIRST, so re-roll if CUDA<13 or cards are short.
name: coder

_comment: REASONING specialist — dense 32B, RL-reasoning-tuned, long chain-of-thought. THE <think> LANDMINE: this model emits a <think>...</think> block BEFORE its answer and the handoff already lost a session to exactly this (qwen3 max_tokens=1024 let the think block eat the budget and truncated the graded answer). So: max_len 16384 (room to think AND answer), and the harness must grade the text AFTER </think>, not the raw stream. If BF16 weights (~64GB) + KV do not fit 80GB at mem_util 0.9, drop to a 4-bit AWQ quant of the same model — that is the only expected OOM here. FLAG NOTE: `--reasoning-parser deepseek_r1` alone is correct for vLLM 0.24 (the old `--enable-reasoning` was removed); it puts the CoT in message.reasoning/reasoning_content and leaves the clean answer in message.content, which is what the harness grades. If the boot lands on the CUDA-12 vllm-0.11 fallback and rejects the flag, boot.sh fails CLOSED (kills the pod), so a bad flag wastes a boot but never serves silently-wrong data.
name: reasoning

_comment: GENERAL specialist — a broad-knowledge instruct model, genuinely NOT code- or math-specialized, as the contrast axis. Pinned to google/gemma-3-27b-it because it is KNOWN-GOOD in this project (modern2 served it) so it carries zero boot risk, is genuinely general, and gated Gemma works via the HF_TOKEN boot.sh reads from /workspace/.hf_token. UPGRADE OPTION (confirm servability at boot, do not guess-boot): swap to GLM-5.2 (the current open all-round frontier) if it fits 1-2 cards — it would make the general axis top-tier rather than merely solid. Not pinned to it here only because its exact size/repo is unverified and a wrong repo wastes a boot.
name: general
